description_long: This object is the core of an apple that has been mostly eaten, with only the central fibrous core and a small amount of flesh remaining. The top and bottom retain some of the original red and yellow apple skin, while the sides are irregularly gnawed, exposing the pale, slightly browned interior. The surface is uneven, with visible bite marks, wrinkles, and some discoloration from oxidation. The apple core appears slightly shriveled and has a rough, organic texture, with some darker spots indicating bruising or decay. The object is small and lightweight, typical of a discarded apple core found in urban environments such as sidewalks or parks.
description: A partially eaten, browned apple core with red-yellow skin and visible bite marks.
description_view_0: Shows the apple core's top with red skin and deep bite marks on the left.
description_view_1: Displays a flatter side with more exposed, browned flesh and fewer skin patches.
description_view_2: Reveals the most skin at the top and a pronounced central cavity.
description_view_3: Highlights the opposite side with a large, dark cavity and less skin.
